content_en:
Salmaan (RA) went to visit Abu Darda (RA) (they were both made brothers when the latter came to Madinah), but he had not yet reached home. Umm Darda (RA) complained to Salmaan (RA) that her husband had no need for her anymore. He fasted during the day and prayed at night. Abu Darda (RA) came and offered food. Salmaan (RA) implored his host to break his fast (since it was a voluntary one) and made him eat with him. He stayed the night with them and prevented Abu Darda (RA) from praying and said: “Your body has a right over you; your Lord has a right over

you and your family has a right over you. So fast and do not fast. Pray and spend some time with your family and give everybody their due right.” When morning came they made ablution and went to the mosque where they narrated the saga to the Prophet (SAW) who said: “Salmaan spoke the truth.”

Perhaps this is sometimes even more necessary for the husband to do than providing his wife with food and clothing. Especially when she has not had any Islamic orientation. This is in line with the practice of the Prophet (SAW), who not only taught his own wives but also allowed a poor man to give his wife education as her mahr.** Many men are quite lazy and indifferent in this matter. May Allah help us.

f. The right to be kept in honour and dignity.

This is one of the most prominent rights of the wife over her husband. The husband should do everything to protect his wife’s honour and dignity particularly in front of other people. There are animals in the jungle who are more considerate of their mates’ honour and pride than humans. Yes, this does mean that there should be a certain amount of possessiveness. The Prophet (SAW) described the brotherin-law as being death (when alone with the brother’s wife), so how can men justify their women mixing and mingling with strangers and others without any supervision. Sa’d

* Bukhari % That was teaching his wife what he knew of the Quran.

(RA) was the most possessive about his wife among the companions. The Prophet (SAW) said: “Are you amazed at how much Sa’d is possessive? By Allah, I am much more possessive than he is and Allah is much more so than I.”*"

So this is very necessary for men. The wolf will always devour the lonely sheep.

The Rights of both Husband and Wife over each other

The third type of right is common between the couple. No one is precluded from these. Those are:

a. Not tosreveal secrets.

This is a general rule for both parties and both will be held equally responsible to uphold the other’s confidentiality. The Prophet (SAW) said: “The man who will be the most wretched in status, in the Eyes of Allah on the Day of Judgement, will be the husband who confides in his wife and she in him, and then he goes and reveals her secret. pes

>” Muslim * Tid

This plays a very big role in the development of marriage and the household in general. Spouses should advise one another and even take account of and check one another. It is a mistake to think that this role is reserved for the husband and the wife should tolerate the mistakes of the husband. Each should point out, with affection, the mistakes of the other and each should accept their own faults.

This applies to the everyday running of the house and family affairs. The husband should not belittle the advice and opinion of his wife, in case her opinion proves to be more practical and correct than his. The best example to follow in this issue is the Prophet (SAW) when he sought the advice of Umm Salamah (RA) at the treaty of Hudaibiyah. The Companions were in no mood for any compromise with the Quraish, but the Prophet (SAW) had, through divine inspiration, signed a peace treaty with the Quraish. The Prophet (SAW) had the difficult task of consoling his Companions and asking them to shave their heads and release their Ihram (Hajj garments). Umm Salamah (RA) advised him to shave his head first, then the Companions would automatically follow him., That is what happened.

d. True love between the couple.

Marriage cannot be successful without this. And although this is an issue which does not follow any fationale, each spouse should at least express love towards the other. The

Prophet (SAW) used to offer words of love and affection towards his wives. We should follow him and not be shy or reluctant about the matter. Allah describes the houris of Paradise as being extremely compassionate (‘urub) towards their spouses. A marriage which is devoid of good and warm words is one that is bereft of any happiness.

* cf Surah al-Waqi’ah (56), ayat 37.

3. Arealistic approach to married life.

This issue can be discussed under the following headings:

a. A realistic attitude towards the mahr, reception and gifts.

There is no benefit in burdening the husband with extravagant mahr and imposing upon him to give a lavish wedding reception. In fact, it could very well be the downfall of many marriages. All expenses should be met according to the husband’s financial capability. The wife (and other members of the family) should not expect the husband to spend in excess merely because he is marrying her.

b. One of the better ingredients for a happy marriage, on the wife’s part, is not to pressure her husband into spending money on things he cannot afford. There should be patience in difficult times and content in prosperous times. The worst thing a woman can do for her marriage is to rebel in money matters and create unnecessary division between them. The Prophet left his wives for a whole month when they demanded better living standards from him. He remained apart from them until Allah revealed:

“O Prophet! Tell your wives: ‘If you desire the life of this world and its lustre then come, I will give you some means and then let you leave gracefully. But if you desire Allah and His Messenger and the abode of the Hereafter, then Allah has prepared, for those who are good among you, a great reward.”

“ Surah al-Ahzaab (33), ayats 28-29.

So the Prophet chose them and they reciprocated.

The husband should also be considerate and be generous in spending. He should avoid being stingy, for that is condemned. Allah says:

“Let the wealthy spend from his wealth. And those for whom Allah has restricted their provisions, they should spend from what ever Allah has given them.”*"

c. Being realistic in expectations and avoiding fantasies.

Very few people live up to this reality. Many couples paint a fantastic picture in their minds before marriage and assume their spouses to be something very similar to the spouses of Paradise. For that, they will have to wait. Moderate expectations from the spouse are commendable and each should try to accommodate the other’s ideals as far as possible. The Prophet (SAW) said: “A believing man should not condemn (a quality of) a believing woman. If he dislikes one characteristic, ‘he may well like another characteristic.”

This is how Allah has created human beings. No one person is endowed with all the best qualities. A woman may be average in beauty but excellent in faith and personality.

If perfectionists stopped and thought for a moment, they would realise that they are unjust. Many of the perfections they seek do not exist in their own lives and personalities. People would like to see certain qualities in them (the perfectionists), just as they would like to find their match. It may happen that a finicky person finds someone he likes

after a very long wait, only to be turned down by the woman because she does not like what she sees. So they end up losing out in life totally.

|. A realistic approach in asking for rights and fulfilment of responsibilities, Although the husband and wife both have rights, it is not proper that they violate each other’s rights and demand

unrealistic chores from each other. They should be very considerate and lighten each other’s burden as much as

4. Understanding the Spouse’s psychological frame of mind.

This is necessary for the compatibility and longevity of marriage. But despite this, many couples do not bother to give this issue any attention. This involves the likes and dislikes of each other and what pleases and displeases each other. Also, this is something which is not understood by merely asking the spouse, but also by observation and insight.

The best example to illustrate this point is the story of Qadi Shuraih who says: “I married a woman from the tribe of Tamim. On the first night I made ablution and offered prayers. She joined in. When I finished, I asked Allah to make her a means of blessings, to grant me pleasure from her good qualities and to protect me from her bad. Then I praised Allah. She said: ‘I am a stranger for you. What ever pleases you I will oblige and what ever displeases you I will avoid.’ So I told her what I liked and disliked. She asked: “Would like my family to visit me?’ I told her that since I was a judge, 1 would bore them. Then she asked me which neighbours I preferred, so T informed her.”

“{ lived with this woman in happiness and bliss for a whole year. Then, an old woman came to the house who was ordering my wife around. On enquiring, I found out that she was my mother-in-law who immediately asked me: “How are you with your wife?’ I said that she is the best wife I could have. The mother said: “There is no worse person than the one who is spoiled. If she puts you in any kind of doubt then hit her.”

“The mother would then come every year to our house and give advice to her daughter. I lived with my wife for twenty years

and only once was I angry at her. And that too was my mistake.”

It is well known that one of the greatest objectives of marriage is the procreation of humans and to continue one’s own genealogy. There are several rights and pre-requisites involved in this matter. I will outline them.

a. Bearing a child.

Many husbands ask their wives to use contraceptive devices immediately after marriage because they want to enjoy them first. This is detrimental for the wife because it is medically proven that when a woman who has never given birth uses contraceptive pills, she is at risk of becoming barren for the rest of her life.
content_id:
Salmaan (RA) pergi mengunjungi Abu Darda (RA) (keduanya dijadikan saudara ketika Abu Darda datang ke Madinah), tetapi ia belum sampai di rumah. Umm Darda (RA) mengeluh kepada Salmaan (RA) bahwa suaminya tidak lagi membutuhkannya. Ia berpuasa di siang hari dan salah di malam hari. Abu Darda (RA) datang dan menawarkan makanan. Salmaan (RA) memohon kepada tuan rumahnya untuk membatalkan puasanya (karena itu adalah puasa sunnah) dan membuatnya makan bersamanya. Ia bermalam bersama mereka dan mencegah Abu Darda (RA) dari salah malam lalu berkata: "Tubuhmu memiliki hak atasmu; Tuhanmu memiliki hak atasmu

dan keluargamu memiliki hak atasmu. Maka berpuasalah dan jangan terus-menerus berpuasa. Salatlah dan luangkan waktu bersama keluargamu serta berikan setiap orang haknya." Ketika pagi tiba, mereka berwudhu dan pergi ke masjid di mana mereka menceritakan kisah tersebut kepada Nabi (SAW) yang bersabda: "Salmaan berkata benar."

Barangkali hal ini terkadang bahkan lebih penting bagi suami untuk dilakukan daripada menyediakan makanan dan pakaian bagi istrinya. Terutama ketika sang istri belum mendapatkan bimbingan Islam. Ini sejalan dengan praktik Nabi (SAW), yang tidak hanya mengajar istri-istri beliau sendiri tetapi juga mengizinkan seorang pria miskin untuk memberikan pendidikan kepada istrinya sebagai mahr-nya.** Banyak pria yang cukup malas dan acuh dalam masalah ini. Semoga Allah menolong kita.

f. Hak untuk dijaga kehormatan dan martabatnya.

Ini adalah salah satu hak istri yang paling menonjol atas suaminya. Suami harus melakukan segala sesuatu untuk melindungi kehormatan dan martabat istrinya, khususnya di hadapan orang lain. Ada binatang di hutan yang lebih memperhatikan kehormatan dan harga diri pasangannya daripada manusia. Ya, ini memang berarti harus ada tingkat kecemburuan tertentu. Nabi (SAW) menggambarkan ipar laki-laki sebagai kematian (ketika berduaan dengan istri saudaranya), lalu bagaimana bisa para pria membenarkan wanita mereka bercampur baur dengan orang asing dan orang lain tanpa pengawasan. Sa'd

* Bukhari % Yaitu mengajarkan kepada istrinya apa yang ia ketahui dari Al-Quran.

(RA) adalah yang paling pencemburu terhadap istrinya di antara para sahabat. Nabi (SAW) bersabda: "Apakah kalian heran dengan betapa cemburunya Sa'd? Demi Allah, aku jauh lebih cemburu darinya dan Allah jauh lebih cemburu dariku."*"

Maka ini sangat penting bagi para pria. Serigala akan selalu memangsa domba yang sendirian.

Hak-hak Bersama antara Suami dan Istri

Jenis hak yang ketiga adalah yang berlaku bersama di antara pasangan. Tidak ada seorang pun yang dikecualikan dari hak-hak ini. Hak-hak tersebut adalah:

a. Tidak membocorkan rahasia.

Ini adalah aturan umum bagi kedua pihak dan keduanya akan dimintai pertanggungjawaban secara setara untuk menjaga kerahasiaan satu sama lain. Nabi (SAW) bersabda: "Orang yang paling buruk kedudukannya di Sisi Allah pada Hari Kiamat adalah suami yang berbagi rahasia dengan istrinya dan istri dengannya, kemudian dia pergi dan membocorkan rahasianya."

" Muslim * Ibid

Hal ini memainkan peran yang sangat besar dalam perkembangan pernikahan dan rumah tangga secara umum. Pasangan suami istri harus saling menasihati dan bahkan saling mengoreksi satu sama lain. Adalah sebuah kesalahan untuk berpikir bahwa peran ini hanya diperuntukkan bagi suami dan istri harus menoleransi kesalahan suami. Masing-masing harus menunjukkan, dengan penuh kasih sayang, kesalahan pihak lain dan masing-masing harus menerima kekurangan mereka sendiri.

Hal ini berlaku untuk pengelolaan rumah tangga dan urusan keluarga sehari-hari. Suami tidak boleh meremehkan nasihat dan pendapat istrinya, karena bisa jadi pendapatnya terbukti lebih praktis dan benar daripada pendapatnya. Contoh terbaik untuk diikuti dalam masalah ini adalah Nabi (SAW) ketika beliau meminta nasihat Umm Salamah (RA) pada Perjanjian Hudaibiyah. Para sahabat sama sekali tidak berminat berkompromi dengan Quraisy, tetapi Nabi (SAW), melalui wahyu ilahi, telah menandatangani perjanjian damai dengan Quraisy. Nabi (SAW) memiliki tugas yang sulit untuk menghibur para sahabat dan meminta mereka mencukur kepala dan melepaskan ihram (pakaian haji) mereka. Umm Salamah (RA) menasihati beliau untuk mencukur kepalanya terlebih dahulu, maka para sahabat akan secara otomatis mengikutinya. Itulah yang terjadi.

d. Cinta yang tulus antara pasangan.

Pernikahan tidak dapat berhasil tanpa hal ini. Dan meskipun ini adalah masalah yang tidak mengikuti logika semata, setiap pasangan setidaknya harus mengungkapkan cinta kepada yang lain. Nabi

(SAW) biasa mengucapkan kata-kata cinta dan kasih sayang kepada istri-istri beliau. Kita harus mengikuti beliau dan tidak malu atau enggan dalam hal ini. Allah menggambarkan bidadari surga sebagai sangat penuh kasih sayang ('urub) terhadap pasangan mereka. Pernikahan yang tidak memiliki kata-kata yang baik dan hangat adalah pernikahan yang kehilangan kebahagiaan.

* lih. Surah al-Waqi'ah (56), ayat 37.

3. Pendekatan yang realistis terhadap kehidupan berumah tangga.

Masalah ini dapat dibahas di bawah judul-judul berikut:

a. Sikap yang realistis terhadap mahr, resepsi, dan hadiah.

Tidak ada manfaatnya membebani suami dengan mahr yang berlebihan dan memaksanya mengadakan resepsi pernikahan yang mewah. Bahkan, hal itu bisa jadi menjadi penyebab kehancuran banyak pernikahan. Semua pengeluaran harus disesuaikan dengan kemampuan finansial suami. Istri (dan anggota keluarga lainnya) tidak boleh mengharapkan suami untuk berbelanja berlebihan hanya karena dia menikahinya.

b. Salah satu bahan yang lebih baik untuk pernikahan yang bahagia, dari pihak istri, adalah tidak menekan suaminya untuk mengeluarkan uang untuk hal-hal yang tidak mampu ia beli. Harus ada kesabaran di masa-masa sulit dan rasa syukur di masa-masa makmur. Hal terburuk yang bisa dilakukan seorang wanita untuk pernikahannya adalah memberontak dalam urusan keuangan dan menciptakan perpecahan yang tidak perlu di antara mereka. Nabi meninggalkan istri-istri beliau selama sebulan penuh ketika mereka menuntut standar hidup yang lebih baik darinya. Beliau tetap terpisah dari mereka hingga Allah menurunkan:

"Wahai Nabi! Katakanlah kepada istri-istrimu: 'Jika kamu menginginkan kehidupan dunia dan perhiasannya, maka marilah, akan kuberikan kepadamu mut'ah dan akan kuceraikan kamu dengan cara yang baik. Tetapi jika kamu menginginkan Allah dan Rasul-Nya serta negeri akhirat, maka sesungguhnya Allah telah menyediakan bagi yang berbuat baik di antara kamu, pahala yang besar.'"

" Surah al-Ahzaab (33), ayat 28-29.

Maka Nabi memilih mereka dan mereka pun membalas pilihan tersebut.

Suami juga harus penuh perhatian dan dermawan dalam berbelanja. Ia harus menghindari sifat kikir, karena hal itu tercela. Allah berfirman:

"Hendaklah orang yang mampu memberi nafkah menurut kemampuannya. Dan orang yang disempitkan rezekinya, hendaklah memberi nafkah dari harta yang diberikan Allah kepadanya."*"

c. Bersikap realistis dalam harapan dan menghindari angan-angan.

Sangat sedikit orang yang hidup sesuai dengan kenyataan ini. Banyak pasangan melukiskan gambaran fantastis dalam pikiran mereka sebelum menikah dan menganggap pasangan mereka akan mirip dengan pasangan-pasangan di surga. Untuk itu, mereka harus menunggu. Harapan yang moderat terhadap pasangan adalah terpuji dan masing-masing harus berusaha mengakomodasi cita-cita pihak lain sejauh mungkin. Nabi (SAW) bersabda: "Seorang mukmin laki-laki tidak boleh membenci seorang mukminah. Jika ia tidak menyukai satu sifatnya, bisa jadi ia menyukai sifat lainnya."

Beginilah Allah menciptakan manusia. Tidak ada satu pun orang yang dikaruniai semua sifat terbaik. Seorang wanita mungkin biasa saja dalam kecantikan tetapi luar biasa dalam iman dan kepribadian.

Jika orang-orang perfeksionis berhenti sejenak dan berpikir, mereka akan menyadari bahwa mereka tidak adil. Banyak kesempurnaan yang mereka cari tidak ada dalam kehidupan dan kepribadian mereka sendiri. Orang-orang ingin melihat sifat-sifat tertentu pada mereka (si perfeksionis), sama seperti mereka ingin menemukan pasangan yang cocok. Bisa jadi seseorang yang terlalu memilih akhirnya menemukan seseorang yang disukainya

setelah menunggu sangat lama, hanya untuk ditolak oleh wanita itu karena dia tidak menyukai apa yang dilihatnya. Sehingga mereka akhirnya rugi total dalam hidup.

|. Pendekatan yang realistis dalam menuntut hak dan pemenuhan tanggung jawab. Meskipun suami dan istri sama-sama memiliki hak, tidaklah pantas mereka melanggar hak satu sama lain dan menuntut

tugas-tugas yang tidak realistis dari satu sama lain. Mereka harus sangat pengertian dan meringankan beban satu sama lain sebisa mungkin.

4. Memahami kondisi psikologis pasangan.

Hal ini diperlukan untuk kecocokan dan kelanggengan pernikahan. Namun terlepas dari itu, banyak pasangan tidak mau repot memberikan perhatian pada masalah ini. Ini melibatkan hal-hal yang disukai dan tidak disukai masing-masing serta apa yang menyenangkan dan tidak menyenangkan satu sama lain. Selain itu, ini adalah sesuatu yang tidak dipahami hanya dengan bertanya kepada pasangan, tetapi juga melalui pengamatan dan wawasan.

Contoh terbaik untuk menggambarkan hal ini adalah kisah Qadi Shuraih yang berkata: "Aku menikahi seorang wanita dari suku Tamim. Pada malam pertama, aku berwudhu dan menunaikan salah. Ia ikut bersamaku. Ketika selesai, aku memohon kepada Allah agar menjadikannya sumber keberkahan, memberiku kesenangan dari sifat-sifat baiknya, dan melindungiku dari sifat-sifat buruknya. Kemudian aku memuji Allah. Ia berkata: 'Aku adalah orang asing bagimu. Apa pun yang menyenangkanmu akan aku penuhi dan apa pun yang tidak menyenangkanmu akan aku hindari.' Maka aku memberitahunya apa yang aku suka dan tidak suka. Ia bertanya: 'Apakah engkau ingin keluargaku mengunjungiku?' Aku menjawab bahwa karena aku seorang hakim, aku akan membuat mereka bosan. Kemudian ia bertanya tetangga mana yang aku sukai, maka aku memberitahunya."

"Aku hidup bersama wanita ini dalam kebahagiaan dan kegembiraan selama setahun penuh. Kemudian, seorang wanita tua datang ke rumah yang memerintah istriku. Setelah bertanya, aku mengetahui bahwa ia adalah ibu mertuaku yang langsung bertanya kepadaku: 'Bagaimana keadaanmu dengan istrimu?' Aku menjawab bahwa ia adalah istri terbaik yang bisa kumiliki. Sang ibu berkata: 'Tidak ada orang yang lebih buruk dari orang yang dimanjakan. Jika ia membuatmu ragu dalam hal apa pun, maka pukullah ia.'"

"Sang ibu kemudian datang setiap tahun ke rumah kami dan memberikan nasihat kepada putrinya. Aku hidup bersama istriku selama dua puluh tahun

dan hanya sekali aku marah kepadanya. Dan itu pun adalah kesalahanku."

Sudah diketahui bahwa salah satu tujuan terbesar pernikahan adalah melanjutkan keturunan manusia dan meneruskan garis keturunan seseorang. Ada beberapa hak dan prasyarat yang terlibat dalam masalah ini. Saya akan menguraikannya.

a. Memiliki anak.

Banyak suami meminta istri mereka menggunakan alat kontrasepsi segera setelah menikah karena mereka ingin menikmati istri mereka terlebih dahulu. Ini merugikan bagi istri karena secara medis terbukti bahwa ketika seorang wanita yang belum pernah melahirkan menggunakan pil kontrasepsi, ia berisiko menjadi mandul seumur hidupnya.
content_ar:
Salmaan (RA) went to visit Abu Darda (RA) (they were both made brothers when the latter came to Madinah), but he had not yet reached home. Umm Darda (RA) complained to Salmaan (RA) that her husband had no need for her anymore. He fasted during the day and prayed at night. Abu Darda (RA) came and offered food. Salmaan (RA) implored his host to break his fast (since it was a voluntary one) and made him eat with him. He stayed the night with them and prevented Abu Darda (RA) from praying and said: “Your body has a right over you; your Lord has a right over

you and your family has a right over you. So fast and do not fast. Pray and spend some time with your family and give everybody their due right.” When morning came they made ablution and went to the mosque where they narrated the saga to النبي ﷺ (SAW) who said: “Salmaan spoke the truth.”

Perhaps this is sometimes even more necessary for the husband to do than providing his wife with food and clothing. Especially when she has not had any Islamic orientation. This is in line with the practice of النبي ﷺ (SAW), who not only taught his own wives but also allowed a poor man to give his wife education as her mahr.** Many men are quite lazy and indifferent in this matter. May الله help us.

f. The right to be kept in honour and dignity.

This is one of the most prominent rights of the wife over her husband. The husband should do everything to protect his wife’s honour and dignity particularly in front of other people. There are animals in the jungle who are more considerate of their mates’ honour and pride than humans. Yes, this does mean that there should be a certain amount of possessiveness. النبي ﷺ (SAW) described the brotherin-law as being death (when alone with the brother’s wife), so how can men justify their women mixing and mingling with strangers and others without any supervision. Sa’d

* Bukhari % That was teaching his wife what he knew of the القرآن الكريم.

(RA) was the most possessive about his wife among the الصحابة. النبي ﷺ (SAW) said: “Are you amazed at how much Sa’d is possessive? By الله, I am much more possessive than he is and الله is much more so than I.”*"

So this is very necessary for men. The wolf will always devour the lonely sheep.

The Rights of both Husband and Wife over each other

The third type of right is common between the couple. No one is precluded from these. Those are:

a. Not tosreveal secrets.

This is a general rule for both parties and both will be held equally responsible to uphold the other’s confidentiality. النبي ﷺ (SAW) said: “The man who will be the most wretched in status, in the Eyes of الله on the Day of Judgement, will be the husband who confides in his wife and she in him, and then he goes and reveals her secret. pes

>” Muslim * Tid

This plays a very big role in the development of marriage and the household in general. Spouses should advise one another and even take account of and check one another. It is a mistake to think that this role is reserved for the husband and the wife should tolerate the mistakes of the husband. Each should point out, with affection, the mistakes of the other and each should accept their own faults.

This applies to the everyday running of the house and family affairs. The husband should not belittle the advice and opinion of his wife, in case her opinion proves to be more practical and correct than his. The best example to follow in this issue is النبي ﷺ (SAW) when he sought the advice of Umm Salamah (RA) at the treaty of Hudaibiyah. The الصحابة were in no mood for any compromise with the Quraish, but النبي ﷺ (SAW) had, through divine inspiration, signed a peace treaty with the Quraish. النبي ﷺ (SAW) had the difficult task of consoling his الصحابة and asking them to shave their heads and release their Ihram (Hajj garments). Umm Salamah (RA) advised him to shave his head first, then the الصحابة would automatically follow him., That is what happened.

d. True love between the couple.

Marriage cannot be successful without this. And although this is an issue which does not follow any fationale, each spouse should at least express love towards the other. The

Prophet (SAW) used to offer words of love and affection towards his wives. We should follow him and not be shy or reluctant about the matter. الله describes the houris of Paradise as being extremely compassionate (‘urub) towards their spouses. A marriage which is devoid of good and warm words is one that is bereft of any happiness.

* cf Surah al-Waqi’ah (56), ayat 37.

3. Arealistic approach to married life.

This issue can be discussed under the following headings:

a. A realistic attitude towards the mahr, reception and gifts.

There is no benefit in burdening the husband with extravagant mahr and imposing upon him to give a lavish wedding reception. In fact, it could very well be the downfall of many marriages. All expenses should be met according to the husband’s financial capability. The wife (and other members of the family) should not expect the husband to spend in excess merely because he is marrying her.

b. One of the better ingredients for a happy marriage, on the wife’s part, is not to pressure her husband into spending money on things he cannot afford. There should be patience in difficult times and content in prosperous times. The worst thing a woman can do for her marriage is to rebel in money matters and create unnecessary division between them. النبي ﷺ left his wives for a whole month when they demanded better living standards from him. He remained apart from them until الله revealed:

“O Prophet! Tell your wives: ‘If you desire the life of this world and its lustre then come, I will give you some means and then let you leave gracefully. But if you desire الله and His Messenger and the abode of the Hereafter, then الله has prepared, for those who are good among you, a great reward.”

“ Surah al-Ahzaab (33), ayats 28-29.

So النبي ﷺ chose them and they reciprocated.

The husband should also be considerate and be generous in spending. He should avoid being stingy, for that is condemned. الله says:

“Let the wealthy spend from his wealth. And those for whom الله has restricted their provisions, they should spend from what ever الله has given them.”*"

c. Being realistic in expectations and avoiding fantasies.

Very few people live up to this reality. Many couples paint a fantastic picture in their minds before marriage and assume their spouses to be something very similar to the spouses of Paradise. For that, they will have to wait. Moderate expectations from the spouse are commendable and each should try to accommodate the other’s ideals as far as possible. النبي ﷺ (SAW) said: “A believing man should not condemn (a quality of) a believing woman. If he dislikes one characteristic, ‘he may well like another characteristic.”

This is how الله has created human beings. No one person is endowed with all the best qualities. A woman may be average in beauty but excellent in faith and personality.

If perfectionists stopped and thought for a moment, they would realise that they are unjust. Many of the perfections they seek do not exist in their own lives and personalities. People would like to see certain qualities in them (the perfectionists), just as they would like to find their match. It may happen that a finicky person finds someone he likes

after a very long wait, only to be turned down by the woman because she does not like what she sees. So they end up losing out in life totally.

|. A realistic approach in asking for rights and fulfilment of responsibilities, Although the husband and wife both have rights, it is not proper that they violate each other’s rights and demand

unrealistic chores from each other. They should be very considerate and lighten each other’s burden as much as

4. Understanding the Spouse’s psychological frame of mind.

This is necessary for the compatibility and longevity of marriage. But despite this, many couples do not bother to give this issue any attention. This involves the likes and dislikes of each other and what pleases and displeases each other. Also, this is something which is not understood by merely asking the spouse, but also by observation and insight.

The best example to illustrate this point is the story of Qadi Shuraih who says: “I married a woman from the tribe of Tamim. On the first night I made ablution and offered prayers. She joined in. When I finished, I asked الله to make her a means of blessings, to grant me pleasure from her good qualities and to protect me from her bad. Then I praised الله. She said: ‘I am a stranger for you. What ever pleases you I will oblige and what ever displeases you I will avoid.’ So I told her what I liked and disliked. She asked: “Would like my family to visit me?’ I told her that since I was a judge, 1 would bore them. Then she asked me which neighbours I preferred, so T informed her.”

“{ lived with this woman in happiness and bliss for a whole year. Then, an old woman came to the house who was ordering my wife around. On enquiring, I found out that she was my mother-in-law who immediately asked me: “How are you with your wife?’ I said that she is the best wife I could have. The mother said: “There is no worse person than the one who is spoiled. If she puts you in any kind of doubt then hit her.”

“The mother would then come every year to our house and give advice to her daughter. I lived with my wife for twenty years

and only once was I angry at her. And that too was my mistake.”

It is well known that one of the greatest objectives of marriage is the procreation of humans and to continue one’s own genealogy. There are several rights and pre-requisites involved in this matter. I will outline them.

a. Bearing a child.

Many husbands ask their wives to use contraceptive devices immediately after marriage because they want to enjoy them first. This is detrimental for the wife because it is medically proven that when a woman who has never given birth uses contraceptive pills, she is at risk of becoming barren for the rest of her life.
